stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care during a safety review in the long-term care unit. The client is a 42-year-old adult client. The RN is assigning care to a licensed practical/vocational nurse. Which assignments are appropriate for the LPN/VN? Select all that apply.
rationale: LPN/VN practice commonly includes medication administration, data collection, reinforcement of teaching, and selected procedures; RN responsibilities include initial assessment, care planning, teaching, triage, and evaluation. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Develop the initial nursing plan of care for a new admission.
Interpret complex assessment trends and revise nursing diagnoses independently.
Provide the first teaching session for a new diagnosis of heart failure.
Perform a sterile dressing change for a stable postoperative client if within scope.
Triage an unstable client reporting crushing chest pain.
Administer prescribed oral medications to stable clients.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care during a care-coordination review in the acute care unit. The focus is several assigned clients. The nurse receives report on several clients. Which situations require priority nursing assessment? Select all that apply.
rationale: Priority assessment is required for airway/breathing compromise, acute abdomen, transfusion reaction, and possible cardiac ischemia. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: A client with new confusion and oxygen saturation below baseline.
A client receiving blood who reports chills and low back pain.
A client with chest pressure unrelieved by rest.
A client requesting help choosing lunch from the menu.
A stable client asking for a scheduled stool softener.
A client who needs routine discharge paperwork after stable vital signs.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while reviewing the care plan in the clinic. The client is a 49-year-old adult client. Which nursing actions protect confidentiality of client health information? Select all that apply.
rationale: Confidentiality requires limiting access and disclosure to authorized persons and care-related need-to-know situations. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Post a client story on social media after removing the name.
Verify authorization before releasing health information to others.
Share test results with a family member who is not authorized because they are worried.
Log off the electronic health record before leaving the workstation.
Discuss client information only with team members involved in the client's care.
Leave a printed report sheet in an unlocked public area.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care during interdisciplinary review in the hospital unit. The client is a 28-year-old adult client. The nurse is giving a change-of-shift report. Which information should be included for safe continuity of care? Select all that apply.
rationale: Effective handoff includes concise, relevant information needed to continue safe care, including status, changes, pending needs, and safety risks. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Pending laboratory or diagnostic results that need follow-up.
Only the medication list because the receiving nurse can read the chart later.
Pertinent assessment changes during the shift.
Speculation about family conflicts unrelated to care.
Personal opinions about the client's personality.
Current code status and allergy information.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while checking care priorities in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 28-year-old adult client. A client is found on the floor after attempting to ambulate without assistance. Which actions should the nurse take? Select all that apply.
rationale: After a fall, assess and treat the client, notify appropriate personnel, document factual findings, and complete internal safety reporting without referencing the report in the medical record. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Move the client immediately before assessing for pain or deformity.
Document objective assessment findings in the medical record.
Discard the incident report after the provider is notified.
Document in the chart that an incident report was completed.
Blame the assisting staff member in the progress note.
Complete an incident/variance report according to policy.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while applying priority-setting principles in the medical-surgical unit. The client is an 88-year-old client. A confused client repeatedly attempts to remove a needed medical device. Which actions are appropriate before and during restraint use? Select all that apply.
rationale: Restraints are last-resort safety interventions requiring orders, monitoring, reassessment, and use of the least restrictive measure. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Release restraints and provide range-of-motion according to policy.
Use a PRN restraint order indefinitely.
Assess circulation, skin integrity, and need for toileting at required intervals.
Keep restraints in place after the unsafe behavior resolves.
Attempt less restrictive alternatives before applying restraints when safe.
Apply restraints for staff convenience when the unit is busy.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while organizing care in the oncology unit. The client is a 56-year-old adult client. A competent client refuses a recommended treatment. Which nursing responses demonstrate advocacy? Select all that apply.
rationale: Advocacy includes supporting informed decision-making, respecting autonomy, communicating concerns, and documenting objectively. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Document the client's statements and teaching provided objectively.
Tell the client the treatment must be accepted because it was ordered.
Respect the client's right to refuse treatment after being informed.
Assess the client's understanding of the proposed treatment and alternatives.
Withhold information about alternatives to prevent confusion.
Document that the client is noncompliant without further assessment.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while checking care priorities in the rehabilitation unit. The client is a 70-year-old adult client. The nurse is planning discharge for a client with new mobility limitations. Which actions support a safe transition home? Select all that apply.
rationale: Discharge planning starts early and includes assessment, interdisciplinary coordination, education, and confirmation that resources are in place. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Provide written instructions only if the client asks.
Avoid involving other disciplines to reduce cost.
Assume family members can provide all care without assessment.
Collaborate with case management for equipment and home health referrals.
Assess the client's home environment and available support.
Delay planning until the day of discharge.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care during client-centered care planning in the acute care unit. The client is a 21-year-old adult client. Which situations require the RN to intervene because the assigned task is outside the team member's role or training? Select all that apply.
rationale: The RN must intervene when tasks require initial teaching, interpretation, care planning, or care of an unstable client. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: A UAP prepares to transfer a client who suddenly reports dizziness and chest pressure.
An LPN/VN administers an oral medication to a stable client within scope.
An LPN/VN initiates a care plan for a newly admitted unstable client.
A UAP reports a low urine output to the RN.
A UAP assists a stable client with hygiene.
A UAP begins teaching a client how to use a new inhaler.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care during nursing care during a unit council. The focus is clients with varied acuity. A unit is reviewing central line-associated bloodstream infection data. Which actions are appropriate quality-improvement activities? Select all that apply.
rationale: Quality improvement uses aggregate data, evidence-based audits, team participation, intervention, and ongoing evaluation rather than blame. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Share aggregate findings with staff and develop an action plan.
Stop collecting data once one improvement meeting is held.
Change practice based only on one anecdotal event.
Compare current infection rates with the unit's baseline data.
Exclude bedside nurses from improvement planning.
Audit adherence to central-line maintenance bundle elements.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while checking care priorities during a team huddle. The focus is the interprofessional team. Two staff members disagree about assignment fairness during a busy shift. Which actions by the charge nurse support effective conflict management? Select all that apply.
rationale: Effective conflict management is timely, private, objective, collaborative, and focused on safe client care. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Ignore the conflict until after several more shifts.
Discuss the disagreement loudly at the nurses' station.
Use threats or humiliation to end the discussion quickly.
Clarify the shared goal of safe client care.
Facilitate a mutually acceptable plan within staffing constraints.
Take sides based on personal preference.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care during interdisciplinary review in the emergency department. The client is a 42-year-old adult client. Which findings or situations require the nurse to follow mandatory reporting requirements? Select all that apply.
rationale: Nurses follow legal and facility reporting requirements for abuse, neglect, communicable diseases, and specific reportable injuries/events. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: A client requesting a second opinion.
Certain communicable diseases as required by law.
Injuries that must be reported under state or facility policy.
A competent adult refusing a recommended medication.
Suspected child abuse or neglect.
A client choosing not to disclose a diagnosis to a friend.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care during client-centered care planning in the emergency department. The focus is a group of clients. During mass-casualty triage, which clients should be categorized for immediate treatment? Select all that apply.
rationale: Disaster triage prioritizes clients who need immediate life-saving treatment and are likely to benefit from available resources. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: A client with a stable sprained ankle requesting an x-ray first.
A client who is emotionally upset but has no physical injury.
A client with airway obstruction that can be opened with positioning.
A client with unsurvivable injuries despite available resources.
A client with severe external bleeding that can be controlled.
A client with respiratory distress and treatable chest injury.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while checking care priorities in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 56-year-old adult client. The nurse receives a new provider order. Which actions are appropriate before implementation? Select all that apply.
rationale: The nurse verifies accuracy, appropriateness, allergies, and clarity before implementing orders and uses read-back as required. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Verify that the order matches the client's current condition and relevant labs.
Implement an unclear order because the provider is busy.
Accept verbal orders for routine convenience when policy prohibits them.
Compare medication orders with allergy information.
Correct a questionable medication dose without notifying the prescriber.
Clarify orders that are incomplete, illegible, or unsafe.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while planning evidence-based care in the primary care clinic. The client is a 21-year-old adult client. Which client needs should prompt the nurse to request or coordinate an interdisciplinary referral? Select all that apply.
rationale: Speech-language pathology, social work, physical therapy, and wound care referrals support complex needs beyond routine nursing care. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Preference for television volume during hospitalization.
A completed immunization record with no questions.
Complex wound requiring specialized dressing recommendations.
Stable vital signs before routine discharge.
Request for another blanket during rounding.
New food insecurity affecting medication adherence.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while preparing education in the hospital unit. The client is a 63-year-old adult client. Which nursing actions support client rights and respectful care? Select all that apply.
rationale: Respectful care protects dignity, privacy, participation, and unbiased treatment. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Leave the call light out of reach for a dependent client.
Make decisions for a competent client to save time.
Include the client in decisions about the plan of care when possible.
Discuss the client's diagnosis with visitors without permission.
Dismiss a client's cultural preference as inconvenient.
Provide privacy during personal care.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while evaluating current client needs in the acute care unit. The focus is a group of clients. The nurse is organizing the first hour of care after report. Which activities should be completed first? Select all that apply.
rationale: Workload organization prioritizes unstable clients, critical data, time-sensitive treatments, and high-risk therapies. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Call dietary about a missing dessert tray.
Update the bulletin board before checking urgent client needs.
Check a critical laboratory value that was reported during handoff.
Restock the supply cart before initial rounds.
Complete routine documentation for stable clients before assessing changes.
Assess the client with a new change in level of consciousness.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while planning evidence-based care in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 49-year-old adult client. A client has an executed advance directive and a do-not-resuscitate order in the medical record. Which nursing actions protect the client's treatment wishes? Select all that apply.
rationale: Advance directives and code-status orders must be verified, documented, and communicated so that care remains consistent with the client's expressed wishes. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Communicate the client's code status during handoff report.
Remove the DNR information from the handoff to avoid upsetting the family.
Delay notifying the provider until after ordered invasive treatment is completed.
Begin chest compressions while waiting for the provider to review the chart.
Inform responding team members of the DNR order if emergency response is activated.
Ask the family to override the advance directive when the client becomes unstable.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care during a care-coordination review in the procedure area. The client is a 70-year-old adult client. The nurse is preparing a client for an invasive procedure. Which actions support valid informed consent? Select all that apply.
rationale: The provider is responsible for explaining the procedure; the nurse verifies understanding, witnesses signature, addresses barriers, and escalates unanswered questions. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Confirm that the provider has explained the procedure, risks, benefits, and alternatives.
Notify the provider if the client says the procedure has not been explained.
Obtain consent from a visitor who is not the legal surrogate.
Tell the client that signing is required because the provider ordered the procedure.
Ask a sedated client to sign the consent because the procedure is scheduled.
Explain the surgical risks independently and have the client sign immediately.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while reviewing current assessment data in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 42-year-old adult client. The registered nurse is delegating care to unlicensed assistive personnel. Which tasks are appropriate to delegate? Select all that apply.
rationale: UAPs may perform predictable tasks for stable clients and report findings; assessment, teaching, evaluation, and sterile/invasive procedures remain the RN's responsibility. options: Complete the initial assessment for a newly admitted client.

Measure and record urine output from a collection hat.
Change the sterile dressing on a new central venous catheter.

A client who needs a routine dressing change due later in the shift.
A client requesting help choosing lunch from the menu.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while planning evidence-based care in the clinic. The client is a 70-year-old adult client. Which nursing actions protect confidentiality of client health information? Select all that apply. Base decisions on current assessment data.
rationale: Confidentiality requires limiting access and disclosure to authorized persons and care-related need-to-know situations. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Post a client story on social media after removing the name.
Share test results with a family member who is not authorized because they are worried.
Leave a printed report sheet in an unlocked public area.
Verify authorization before releasing health information to others.
Discuss client information only with team members involved in the client's care.
Log off the electronic health record before leaving the workstation.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care during interdisciplinary review in the hospital unit. The client is a 63-year-old adult client. The nurse is giving a change-of-shift report. Which information should be included for safe continuity of care? Select all that apply.
rationale: Effective handoff includes concise, relevant information needed to continue safe care, including status, changes, pending needs, and safety risks. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Pending laboratory or diagnostic results that need follow-up.
Speculation about family conflicts unrelated to care.
Pertinent assessment changes during the shift.
Current code status and allergy information.
Details from another client's medical record.
Only the medication list because the receiving nurse can read the chart later.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care during nursing care in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 21-year-old adult client. A client is found on the floor after attempting to ambulate without assistance. Which actions should the nurse take? Select all that apply.
rationale: After a fall, assess and treat the client, notify appropriate personnel, document factual findings, and complete internal safety reporting without referencing the report in the medical record. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Complete an incident/variance report according to policy.
Document in the chart that an incident report was completed.
Notify the provider according to facility policy.
Assess the client for injury before moving the client if injury is suspected.
Blame the assisting staff member in the progress note.
Discard the incident report after the provider is notified.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care during client-centered care planning in the medical-surgical unit. The client is an 68-year-old client. A confused client repeatedly attempts to remove a needed medical device. Which actions are appropriate before and during restraint use? Select all that apply.
rationale: Restraints are last-resort safety interventions requiring orders, monitoring, reassessment, and use of the least restrictive measure. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Tie restraints to the side rail to keep the client in one position.
Use a PRN restraint order indefinitely.
Attempt less restrictive alternatives before applying restraints when safe.
Use restraints only with a valid order and ongoing reassessment.
Keep restraints in place after the unsafe behavior resolves.
Assess circulation, skin integrity, and need for toileting at required intervals.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while considering risk reduction in the oncology unit. The client is a 63-year-old adult client. A competent client refuses a recommended treatment. Which nursing responses demonstrate advocacy? Select all that apply.
rationale: Advocacy includes supporting informed decision-making, respecting autonomy, communicating concerns, and documenting objectively. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Document that the client is noncompliant without further assessment.
Respect the client's right to refuse treatment after being informed.
Notify the provider that the client wants to discuss concerns before deciding.
Withhold information about alternatives to prevent confusion.
Ask family members to pressure the client into agreeing.
Tell the client the treatment must be accepted because it was ordered.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while planning evidence-based care in the rehabilitation unit. The client is a 49-year-old adult client. The nurse is planning discharge for a client with new mobility limitations. Which actions support a safe transition home? Select all that apply.
rationale: Discharge planning starts early and includes assessment, interdisciplinary coordination, education, and confirmation that resources are in place. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Assess the client's home environment and available support.
Confirm transportation and access to prescribed therapies.
Avoid involving other disciplines to reduce cost.
Assume family members can provide all care without assessment.
Provide written instructions only if the client asks.
Teach medication and follow-up instructions using teach-back.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while applying priority-setting principles in the acute care unit. The client is a 42-year-old adult client. Which situations require the RN to intervene because the assigned task is outside the team member's role or training? Select all that apply.
rationale: The RN must intervene when tasks require initial teaching, interpretation, care planning, or care of an unstable client. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: An LPN/VN reinforces previously taught wound-care instructions.
A UAP assists a stable client with hygiene.
A UAP offers to interpret a newly abnormal cardiac rhythm strip.
An LPN/VN administers an oral medication to a stable client within scope.
A UAP reports a low urine output to the RN.
An LPN/VN initiates a care plan for a newly admitted unstable client.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while planning evidence-based care during a unit council. The focus is a group of clients. A unit is reviewing central line-associated bloodstream infection data. Which actions are appropriate quality-improvement activities? Select all that apply.
rationale: Quality improvement uses aggregate data, evidence-based audits, team participation, intervention, and ongoing evaluation rather than blame. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Stop collecting data once one improvement meeting is held.
Evaluate whether interventions reduce infection rates over time.
Exclude bedside nurses from improvement planning.
Publicly identify nurses associated with each infection at staff meeting.
Change practice based only on one anecdotal event.
Share aggregate findings with staff and develop an action plan.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care while reviewing current assessment data during a team huddle. The focus is the interprofessional team. Two staff members disagree about assignment fairness during a busy shift. Which actions by the charge nurse support effective conflict management? Select all that apply.
rationale: Effective conflict management is timely, private, objective, collaborative, and focused on safe client care. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Discuss the disagreement loudly at the nurses' station.
Clarify the shared goal of safe client care.
Review objective workload and acuity data.
Take sides based on personal preference.
Use threats or humiliation to end the discussion quickly.
Ignore the conflict until after several more shifts.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care during a safety review in the emergency department. The client is a 63-year-old adult client. Which findings or situations require the nurse to follow mandatory reporting requirements? Select all that apply.
rationale: Nurses follow legal and facility reporting requirements for abuse, neglect, communicable diseases, and specific reportable injuries/events. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Suspected elder abuse in a dependent adult.
A competent adult refusing a recommended medication.
Certain communicable diseases as required by law.
Suspected child abuse or neglect.

A client choosing not to disclose a diagnosis to a friend.

stem: The nurse is reviewing foundational care during client-centered care planning in the emergency department. The focus is several assigned clients. During mass-casualty triage, which clients should be categorized for immediate treatment? Select all that apply.

options: A client with airway obstruction that can be opened with positioning.
A client who is emotionally upset but has no physical injury.
A client with unsurvivable injuries despite available resources.
A client with respiratory distress and treatable chest injury.
A client with signs of shock but a likely chance of survival with prompt care.
A client with minor abrasions who can walk to the treatment area.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care while reviewing the care plan in the hospital unit. The client is a 28-year-old adult client. Which nursing actions support client rights and respectful care? Select all that apply.

Leave the call light out of reach for a dependent client.
Ask how the client prefers to be addressed.
Dismiss a client's cultural preference as inconvenient.

Provide privacy during personal care.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care while reviewing current assessment data in the acute care unit. The focus is several assigned clients. The nurse is organizing the first hour of care after report. Which activities should be completed first? Select all that apply.
rationale: Workload organization prioritizes unstable clients, critical data, time-sensitive treatments, and high-risk therapies. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.

Restock the supply cart before initial rounds.
Complete routine documentation for stable clients before assessing changes.
Verify patency of an IV line infusing a high-alert medication.

Assess the client with a new change in level of consciousness.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care while evaluating current client needs in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 21-year-old adult client. Verify the order and advance directive are visible in the medical record.
Begin chest compressions while waiting for the provider to review the chart.
Communicate the client's code status during handoff report.
Notify the provider if a new order conflicts with the advance directive.
Remove the DNR information from the handoff to avoid upsetting the family.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care while considering risk reduction in the procedure area. The client is a 21-year-old adult client. The nurse is preparing a client for an invasive procedure. Which actions support valid informed consent? Select all that apply.
rationale: The provider is responsible for explaining the procedure; the nurse verifies understanding, witnesses signature, addresses barriers, and escalates unanswered questions. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.

Notify the provider if the client says the procedure has not been explained.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care during nursing care in the hospital unit. The client is a 34-year-old adult client. Which nursing actions support client rights and respectful care? Select all that apply.

options: Discuss the client's diagnosis with visitors without permission.
Provide privacy during personal care.
Include the client in decisions about the plan of care when possible.
Ask how the client prefers to be addressed.
Use unbiased language when documenting and communicating.
Dismiss a client's cultural preference as inconvenient.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care during nursing care in the acute care unit. The focus is a group of clients. The nurse is organizing the first hour of care after report. Which activities should be completed first? Select all that apply.
rationale: Workload organization prioritizes unstable clients, critical data, time-sensitive treatments, and high-risk therapies. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Check a critical laboratory value that was reported during handoff.
Administer time-critical medication prescribed for sepsis.
Restock the supply cart before initial rounds.
Verify patency of an IV line infusing a high-alert medication.
Complete routine documentation for stable clients before assessing changes.
Call dietary about a missing dessert tray.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care while reviewing the care plan in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 63-year-old adult client. A client has an executed advance directive and a do-not-resuscitate order in the medical record. Which nursing actions protect the client's treatment wishes? Select all that apply.
rationale: Advance directives and code-status orders must be verified, documented, and communicated so that care remains consistent with the client's expressed wishes. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Begin chest compressions while waiting for the provider to review the chart.
Delay notifying the provider until after ordered invasive treatment is completed.
Remove the DNR information from the handoff to avoid upsetting the family.
Verify the order and advance directive are visible in the medical record.
Notify the provider if a new order conflicts with the advance directive.
Communicate the client's code status during handoff report.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care during client-centered care planning in the procedure area. The client is a 49-year-old adult client. The nurse is preparing a client for an invasive procedure. Which actions support valid informed consent? Select all that apply.
rationale: The provider is responsible for explaining the procedure; the nurse verifies understanding, witnesses signature, addresses barriers, and escalates unanswered questions. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Tell the client that signing is required because the provider ordered the procedure.
Use a qualified medical interpreter when language barriers exist.
Notify the provider if the client says the procedure has not been explained.
Obtain consent from a visitor who is not the legal surrogate.
Explain the surgical risks independently and have the client sign immediately.
Confirm that the provider has explained the procedure, risks, benefits, and alternatives.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care while planning evidence-based care in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 42-year-old adult client. The registered nurse is delegating care to unlicensed assistive personnel. Which tasks are appropriate to delegate? Select all that apply.
rationale: UAPs may perform predictable tasks for stable clients and report findings; assessment, teaching, evaluation, and sterile/invasive procedures remain the RN's responsibility. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Measure and record urine output from a collection hat.
Assist a stable client with ambulation using a gait belt.
Report new shortness of breath or chest pain immediately.
Evaluate whether pain medication was effective.
Teach a client how to self-administer insulin.
Obtain routine vital signs for a stable client.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care during a care-coordination review in the long-term care unit. The client is a 70-year-old adult client. The RN is assigning care to a licensed practical/vocational nurse. Which assignments are appropriate for the LPN/VN? Select all that apply.
rationale: LPN/VN practice commonly includes medication administration, data collection, reinforcement of teaching, and selected procedures; RN responsibilities include initial assessment, care planning, teaching, triage, and evaluation. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Administer prescribed oral medications to stable clients.
Reinforce teaching previously provided by the RN.
Perform a sterile dressing change for a stable postoperative client if within scope.
Provide the first teaching session for a new diagnosis of heart failure.
Collect data on a stable client with a chronic wound.
Interpret complex assessment trends and revise nursing diagnoses independently.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care during nursing care in the acute care unit. The focus is clients with varied acuity. The nurse receives report on several clients. Which situations require priority nursing assessment? Select all that apply.
rationale: Priority assessment is required for airway/breathing compromise, acute abdomen, transfusion reaction, and possible cardiac ischemia. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: A postoperative client with a rigid abdomen and increasing pain.
A client with chest pressure unrelieved by rest.
A client receiving blood who reports chills and low back pain.
A client with new confusion and oxygen saturation below baseline.
A client who needs routine discharge paperwork after stable vital signs.
A client requesting help choosing lunch from the menu.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care while reviewing the care plan in the clinic. The client is a 56-year-old adult client. Which nursing actions protect confidentiality of client health information? Select all that apply.
rationale: Confidentiality requires limiting access and disclosure to authorized persons and care-related need-to-know situations. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Use private areas for sensitive conversations when possible.
Discuss client information only with team members involved in the client's care.
Discuss a client's diagnosis in the elevator if no name is used.

Post a client story on social media after removing the name.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care while reviewing current assessment data in the hospital unit. The client is a 34-year-old adult client. The nurse is giving a change-of-shift report. Which information should be included for safe continuity of care? Select all that apply.

Pertinent assessment changes during the shift.

Details from another client's medical record.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care during nursing care in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 63-year-old adult client. A client is found on the floor after attempting to ambulate without assistance. Which actions should the nurse take? Select all that apply.

Document objective assessment findings in the medical record.
Notify the provider according to facility policy.

Discard the incident report after the provider is notified.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care while evaluating current client needs in the medical-surgical unit. The client is an 88-year-old client. Tie restraints to the side rail to keep the client in one position.
Use a PRN restraint order indefinitely.

Assess circulation, skin integrity, and need for toileting at required intervals.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care during client-centered care planning in the oncology unit. The client is a 70-year-old adult client. A competent client refuses a recommended treatment. Which nursing responses demonstrate advocacy? Select all that apply.

stem: The nurse is planning routine care during nursing care during a unit council. The focus is a group of clients. A unit is reviewing central line-associated bloodstream infection data. Which actions are appropriate quality-improvement activities? Select all that apply.

stem: The nurse applies clinical judgment while preparing education during a team huddle. The focus is the nursing team. Two staff members disagree about assignment fairness during a busy shift. Which actions by the charge nurse support effective conflict management? Select all that apply.

stem: The nurse applies clinical judgment during nursing care in the emergency department. The client is a 56-year-old adult client. Which findings or situations require the nurse to follow mandatory reporting requirements? Select all that apply.

options: Injuries that must be reported under state or facility policy.

stem: The nurse applies clinical judgment during routine clinical judgment review in the emergency department. The focus is a group of clients. During mass-casualty triage, which clients should be categorized for immediate treatment? Select all that apply.

A client with minor abrasions who can walk to the treatment area.
A client with severe external bleeding that can be controlled.

A client with respiratory distress and treatable chest injury.

stem: The nurse applies clinical judgment during a care-coordination review in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 42-year-old adult client. The nurse receives a new provider order. Which actions are appropriate before implementation? Select all that apply.

options: Ignore an allergy alert if the medication is commonly prescribed.
Clarify orders that are incomplete, illegible, or unsafe.
Compare medication orders with allergy information.

Correct a questionable medication dose without notifying the prescriber.

stem: The nurse applies clinical judgment while applying priority-setting principles in the primary care clinic. The client is a 63-year-old adult client. Which client needs should prompt the nurse to request or coordinate an interdisciplinary referral? Select all that apply.

options: Stable vital signs before routine discharge.

Complex wound requiring specialized dressing recommendations.

stem: The nurse applies clinical judgment while evaluating current client needs in the hospital unit. The client is a 49-year-old adult client. Which nursing actions support client rights and respectful care? Select all that apply.

stem: The nurse applies clinical judgment while reviewing the care plan in the acute care unit. The focus is several assigned clients. The nurse is organizing the first hour of care after report. Which activities should be completed first? Select all that apply.

stem: The nurse applies clinical judgment during a care-coordination review in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 49-year-old adult client. options: A client with unsurvivable injuries despite available resources.
A client with airway obstruction that can be opened with positioning.
A client with respiratory distress and treatable chest injury.
A client with minor abrasions who can walk to the treatment area.
A client who is emotionally upset but has no physical injury.

stem: The nurse applies clinical judgment while organizing care in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 49-year-old adult client. The nurse receives a new provider order. Which actions are appropriate before implementation? Select all that apply.

options: Compare medication orders with allergy information.
Verify that the order matches the client's current condition and relevant labs.
Accept verbal orders for routine convenience when policy prohibits them.
Clarify orders that are incomplete, illegible, or unsafe.

Implement an unclear order because the provider is busy.

stem: The nurse applies clinical judgment while planning evidence-based care in the primary care clinic. The client is a 34-year-old adult client. Which client needs should prompt the nurse to request or coordinate an interdisciplinary referral? Select all that apply.

options: Complex wound requiring specialized dressing recommendations.

Need for gait training after a fall.
Stable vital signs before routine discharge.
Difficulty swallowing after a stroke.

stem: The nurse applies clinical judgment during client-centered care planning in the hospital unit. The client is a 34-year-old adult client. Which nursing actions support client rights and respectful care? Select all that apply.

Leave the call light out of reach for a dependent client.
Provide privacy during personal care.

stem: The nurse applies clinical judgment while checking care priorities in the acute care unit. The focus is clients with varied acuity. The nurse is organizing the first hour of care after report. Which activities should be completed first? Select all that apply.

stem: The nurse applies clinical judgment while evaluating current client needs in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 70-year-old adult client. stem: The nurse applies clinical judgment during client-centered care planning in the procedure area. The client is a 49-year-old adult client. The nurse is preparing a client for an invasive procedure. Which actions support valid informed consent? Select all that apply.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while evaluating current client needs during a unit council. The focus is clients with varied acuity. rationale: Quality improvement uses aggregate data, evidence-based audits, team participation, intervention, and ongoing evaluation rather than blame. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Evaluate whether interventions reduce infection rates over time.
Compare current infection rates with the unit's baseline data.
Share aggregate findings with staff and develop an action plan.
Publicly identify nurses associated with each infection at staff meeting.
Change practice based only on one anecdotal event.
Audit adherence to central-line maintenance bundle elements.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety during a safety review during a team huddle. The focus is the nursing team. Two staff members disagree about assignment fairness during a busy shift. Which actions by the charge nurse support effective conflict management? Select all that apply.
rationale: Effective conflict management is timely, private, objective, collaborative, and focused on safe client care. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Listen to each person's concerns in a private setting when possible.
Take sides based on personal preference.
Review objective workload and acuity data.
Clarify the shared goal of safe client care.
Use threats or humiliation to end the discussion quickly.
Facilitate a mutually acceptable plan within staffing constraints.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety during interdisciplinary review in the emergency department. The client is a 21-year-old adult client. Which findings or situations require the nurse to follow mandatory reporting requirements? Select all that apply.
rationale: Nurses follow legal and facility reporting requirements for abuse, neglect, communicable diseases, and specific reportable injuries/events. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Certain communicable diseases as required by law.
A family member asking for a printed medication list.
Injuries that must be reported under state or facility policy.
Suspected child abuse or neglect.
A competent adult refusing a recommended medication.
Suspected elder abuse in a dependent adult.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while planning evidence-based care in the emergency department. The focus is a group of clients. During mass-casualty triage, which clients should be categorized for immediate treatment? Select all that apply.
rationale: Disaster triage prioritizes clients who need immediate life-saving treatment and are likely to benefit from available resources. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: A client who is emotionally upset but has no physical injury.
A client with signs of shock but a likely chance of survival with prompt care.
A client with severe external bleeding that can be controlled.
A client with airway obstruction that can be opened with positioning.
A client with unsurvivable injuries despite available resources.
A client with respiratory distress and treatable chest injury.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while reviewing the care plan in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 70-year-old adult client. The nurse receives a new provider order. Which actions are appropriate before implementation? Select all that apply.
rationale: The nurse verifies accuracy, appropriateness, allergies, and clarity before implementing orders and uses read-back as required. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Compare medication orders with allergy information.
Use read-back for verbal or telephone orders according to policy.
Clarify orders that are incomplete, illegible, or unsafe.
Correct a questionable medication dose without notifying the prescriber.
Verify that the order matches the client's current condition and relevant labs.
Accept verbal orders for routine convenience when policy prohibits them.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while preparing education in the primary care clinic. The client is a 34-year-old adult client. Which client needs should prompt the nurse to request or coordinate an interdisciplinary referral? Select all that apply.
rationale: Speech-language pathology, social work, physical therapy, and wound care referrals support complex needs beyond routine nursing care. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Need for gait training after a fall.
New food insecurity affecting medication adherence.
A completed immunization record with no questions.
Request for another blanket during rounding.
Complex wound requiring specialized dressing recommendations.
Difficulty swallowing after a stroke.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while reviewing current assessment data in the hospital unit. The client is a 63-year-old adult client. Which nursing actions support client rights and respectful care? Select all that apply.
rationale: Respectful care protects dignity, privacy, participation, and unbiased treatment. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Use unbiased language when documenting and communicating.
Make decisions for a competent client to save time.
Ask how the client prefers to be addressed.
Dismiss a client's cultural preference as inconvenient.
Include the client in decisions about the plan of care when possible.
Provide privacy during personal care.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while reviewing the care plan in the acute care unit. The focus is a group of clients. The nurse is organizing the first hour of care after report. Which activities should be completed first? Select all that apply.
rationale: Workload organization prioritizes unstable clients, critical data, time-sensitive treatments, and high-risk therapies. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Restock the supply cart before initial rounds.
Check a critical laboratory value that was reported during handoff.
Update the bulletin board before checking urgent client needs.
Administer time-critical medication prescribed for sepsis.
Assess the client with a new change in level of consciousness.
Verify patency of an IV line infusing a high-alert medication.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety during a care-coordination review in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 28-year-old adult client. A client has an executed advance directive and a do-not-resuscitate order in the medical record. Which nursing actions protect the client's treatment wishes? Select all that apply.
rationale: Advance directives and code-status orders must be verified, documented, and communicated so that care remains consistent with the client's expressed wishes. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Begin chest compressions while waiting for the provider to review the chart.
Inform responding team members of the DNR order if emergency response is activated.
Communicate the client's code status during handoff report.
Notify the provider if a new order conflicts with the advance directive.
Verify the order and advance directive are visible in the medical record.
Remove the DNR information from the handoff to avoid upsetting the family.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while evaluating current client needs in the procedure area. The client is a 70-year-old adult client. The nurse is preparing a client for an invasive procedure. Which actions support valid informed consent? Select all that apply.
rationale: The provider is responsible for explaining the procedure; the nurse verifies understanding, witnesses signature, addresses barriers, and escalates unanswered questions. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Obtain consent from a visitor who is not the legal surrogate.
Notify the provider if the client says the procedure has not been explained.
Tell the client that signing is required because the provider ordered the procedure.
Confirm that the provider has explained the procedure, risks, benefits, and alternatives.
Witness the client's signature only after the client states understanding.
Use a qualified medical interpreter when language barriers exist.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety during client-centered care planning in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 28-year-old adult client. The registered nurse is delegating care to unlicensed assistive personnel. Which tasks are appropriate to delegate? Select all that apply.
rationale: UAPs may perform predictable tasks for stable clients and report findings; assessment, teaching, evaluation, and sterile/invasive procedures remain the RN's responsibility. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Assist a stable client with ambulation using a gait belt.
Teach a client how to self-administer insulin.
Complete the initial assessment for a newly admitted client.
Obtain routine vital signs for a stable client.
Report new shortness of breath or chest pain immediately.
Measure and record urine output from a collection hat.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety during routine clinical judgment review in the long-term care unit. The client is a 42-year-old adult client. The RN is assigning care to a licensed practical/vocational nurse. Which assignments are appropriate for the LPN/VN? Select all that apply.
rationale: LPN/VN practice commonly includes medication administration, data collection, reinforcement of teaching, and selected procedures; RN responsibilities include initial assessment, care planning, teaching, triage, and evaluation. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Perform a sterile dressing change for a stable postoperative client if within scope.
Provide the first teaching session for a new diagnosis of heart failure.
Develop the initial nursing plan of care for a new admission.
Administer prescribed oral medications to stable clients.
Reinforce teaching previously provided by the RN.
Collect data on a stable client with a chronic wound.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while considering risk reduction in the acute care unit. The focus is clients with varied acuity. The nurse receives report on several clients. Which situations require priority nursing assessment? Select all that apply.
rationale: Priority assessment is required for airway/breathing compromise, acute abdomen, transfusion reaction, and possible cardiac ischemia. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: A client with new confusion and oxygen saturation below baseline.
A client receiving blood who reports chills and low back pain.
A client who needs routine discharge paperwork after stable vital signs.
A stable client asking for a scheduled stool softener.
A client with chest pressure unrelieved by rest.
A postoperative client with a rigid abdomen and increasing pain.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety during interdisciplinary review in the clinic. The client is a 28-year-old adult client. Which nursing actions protect confidentiality of client health information? Select all that apply.
rationale: Confidentiality requires limiting access and disclosure to authorized persons and care-related need-to-know situations. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Log off the electronic health record before leaving the workstation.
Share test results with a family member who is not authorized because they are worried.
Post a client story on social media after removing the name.
Verify authorization before releasing health information to others.
Leave a printed report sheet in an unlocked public area.
Use private areas for sensitive conversations when possible.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety during a care-coordination review in the hospital unit. The client is a 34-year-old adult client. The nurse is giving a change-of-shift report. Which information should be included for safe continuity of care? Select all that apply.
rationale: Effective handoff includes concise, relevant information needed to continue safe care, including status, changes, pending needs, and safety risks. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Current code status and allergy information.
Only the medication list because the receiving nurse can read the chart later.
Speculation about family conflicts unrelated to care.
Pending laboratory or diagnostic results that need follow-up.
Pertinent assessment changes during the shift.
Safety concerns such as fall risk or swallowing precautions.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while evaluating current client needs in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 70-year-old adult client. A client is found on the floor after attempting to ambulate without assistance. Which actions should the nurse take? Select all that apply.
rationale: After a fall, assess and treat the client, notify appropriate personnel, document factual findings, and complete internal safety reporting without referencing the report in the medical record. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Document objective assessment findings in the medical record.
Notify the provider according to facility policy.
Complete an incident/variance report according to policy.
Move the client immediately before assessing for pain or deformity.
Document in the chart that an incident report was completed.
Discard the incident report after the provider is notified.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while reviewing current assessment data in the medical-surgical unit. The client is an 88-year-old client. A confused client repeatedly attempts to remove a needed medical device. Which actions are appropriate before and during restraint use? Select all that apply.
rationale: Restraints are last-resort safety interventions requiring orders, monitoring, reassessment, and use of the least restrictive measure. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Release restraints and provide range-of-motion according to policy.
Attempt less restrictive alternatives before applying restraints when safe.
Apply restraints for staff convenience when the unit is busy.
Tie restraints to the side rail to keep the client in one position.
Use restraints only with a valid order and ongoing reassessment.
Use a PRN restraint order indefinitely.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety during interdisciplinary review in the oncology unit. The client is a 28-year-old adult client. A competent client refuses a recommended treatment. Which nursing responses demonstrate advocacy? Select all that apply.
rationale: Advocacy includes supporting informed decision-making, respecting autonomy, communicating concerns, and documenting objectively. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Document the client's statements and teaching provided objectively.
Document that the client is noncompliant without further assessment.
Respect the client's right to refuse treatment after being informed.
Assess the client's understanding of the proposed treatment and alternatives.
Tell the client the treatment must be accepted because it was ordered.
Withhold information about alternatives to prevent confusion.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while considering risk reduction in the rehabilitation unit. The client is a 70-year-old adult client. The nurse is planning discharge for a client with new mobility limitations. Which actions support a safe transition home? Select all that apply.
rationale: Discharge planning starts early and includes assessment, interdisciplinary coordination, education, and confirmation that resources are in place. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Assume family members can provide all care without assessment.
Confirm transportation and access to prescribed therapies.
Assess the client's home environment and available support.
Collaborate with case management for equipment and home health referrals.
Provide written instructions only if the client asks.
Teach medication and follow-up instructions using teach-back.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while applying priority-setting principles in the acute care unit. The client is a 70-year-old adult client. Which situations require the RN to intervene because the assigned task is outside the team member's role or training? Select all that apply.
rationale: The RN must intervene when tasks require initial teaching, interpretation, care planning, or care of an unstable client. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: A UAP begins teaching a client how to use a new inhaler.
An LPN/VN administers an oral medication to a stable client within scope.
An LPN/VN initiates a care plan for a newly admitted unstable client.
A UAP reports a low urine output to the RN.
A UAP prepares to transfer a client who suddenly reports dizziness and chest pressure.
A UAP offers to interpret a newly abnormal cardiac rhythm strip.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while planning evidence-based care in the emergency department. The client is a 21-year-old adult client. Which findings or situations require the nurse to follow mandatory reporting requirements? Select all that apply.
rationale: Nurses follow legal and facility reporting requirements for abuse, neglect, communicable diseases, and specific reportable injuries/events. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: A competent adult refusing a recommended medication.
Certain communicable diseases as required by law.
Suspected elder abuse in a dependent adult.
Suspected child abuse or neglect.
A client requesting a second opinion.
Injuries that must be reported under state or facility policy.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while evaluating current client needs in the emergency department. The focus is clients with varied acuity. During mass-casualty triage, which clients should be categorized for immediate treatment? Select all that apply.
rationale: Disaster triage prioritizes clients who need immediate life-saving treatment and are likely to benefit from available resources. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: A client with minor abrasions who can walk to the treatment area.
A client with severe external bleeding that can be controlled.
A client with signs of shock but a likely chance of survival with prompt care.
A client with a stable sprained ankle requesting an x-ray first.
A client who is emotionally upset but has no physical injury.
A client with respiratory distress and treatable chest injury.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while evaluating current client needs in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 34-year-old adult client. The nurse receives a new provider order. Which actions are appropriate before implementation? Select all that apply.
rationale: The nurse verifies accuracy, appropriateness, allergies, and clarity before implementing orders and uses read-back as required. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Accept verbal orders for routine convenience when policy prohibits them.
Clarify orders that are incomplete, illegible, or unsafe.
Correct a questionable medication dose without notifying the prescriber.
Verify that the order matches the client's current condition and relevant labs.
Compare medication orders with allergy information.
Ignore an allergy alert if the medication is commonly prescribed.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety during a safety review in the primary care clinic. The client is a 49-year-old adult client. Which client needs should prompt the nurse to request or coordinate an interdisciplinary referral? Select all that apply.
rationale: Speech-language pathology, social work, physical therapy, and wound care referrals support complex needs beyond routine nursing care. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: A completed immunization record with no questions.
Request for another blanket during rounding.
Difficulty swallowing after a stroke.
New food insecurity affecting medication adherence.
Complex wound requiring specialized dressing recommendations.
Need for gait training after a fall.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety during a safety review in the hospital unit. The client is a 21-year-old adult client. Which nursing actions support client rights and respectful care? Select all that apply.

options: Dismiss a client's cultural preference as inconvenient.
Include the client in decisions about the plan of care when possible.
Ask how the client prefers to be addressed.
Make decisions for a competent client to save time.
Provide privacy during personal care.
Use unbiased language when documenting and communicating.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while checking care priorities in the acute care unit. The focus is clients with varied acuity. The nurse is organizing the first hour of care after report. Which activities should be completed first? Select all that apply.

options: Verify patency of an IV line infusing a high-alert medication.
Administer time-critical medication prescribed for sepsis.
Call dietary about a missing dessert tray.
Restock the supply cart before initial rounds.
Assess the client with a new change in level of consciousness.
Check a critical laboratory value that was reported during handoff.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety during interdisciplinary review in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 21-year-old adult client. A client has an executed advance directive and a do-not-resuscitate order in the medical record. Which nursing actions protect the client's treatment wishes? Select all that apply.
rationale: Advance directives and code-status orders must be verified, documented, and communicated so that care remains consistent with the client's expressed wishes. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Begin chest compressions while waiting for the provider to review the chart.
Notify the provider if a new order conflicts with the advance directive.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while reviewing the care plan in the procedure area. The client is a 28-year-old adult client. The nurse is preparing a client for an invasive procedure. Which actions support valid informed consent? Select all that apply.
rationale: The provider is responsible for explaining the procedure; the nurse verifies understanding, witnesses signature, addresses barriers, and escalates unanswered questions. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Confirm that the provider has explained the procedure, risks, benefits, and alternatives.
Witness the client's signature only after the client states understanding.
Explain the surgical risks independently and have the client sign immediately.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while evaluating current client needs in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 28-year-old adult client. The registered nurse is delegating care to unlicensed assistive personnel. Which tasks are appropriate to delegate? Select all that apply.

Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.

Complete the initial assessment for a newly admitted client.
Obtain routine vital signs for a stable client.
Assist a stable client with ambulation using a gait belt.
Report new shortness of breath or chest pain immediately.
Evaluate whether pain medication was effective.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety during client-centered care planning in the long-term care unit. The client is a 70-year-old adult client. The RN is assigning care to a licensed practical/vocational nurse. Which assignments are appropriate for the LPN/VN? Select all that apply.

Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while planning evidence-based care in the acute care unit. The focus is clients with varied acuity. The nurse receives report on several clients. Which situations require priority nursing assessment? Select all that apply.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while evaluating current client needs in the clinic. The client is a 21-year-old adult client. Which nursing actions protect confidentiality of client health information? Select all that apply.

Log off the electronic health record before leaving the workstation.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety during routine clinical judgment review in the hospital unit. The client is a 42-year-old adult client. The nurse is giving a change-of-shift report. Which information should be included for safe continuity of care? Select all that apply.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety during nursing care in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 70-year-old adult client. A client is found on the floor after attempting to ambulate without assistance. Which actions should the nurse take? Select all that apply.

stem: The nurse prioritizes care and safety while applying priority-setting principles in the medical-surgical unit. The client is an 72-year-old client. Speculation about family conflicts unrelated to care.
Only the medication list because the receiving nurse can read the chart later.

stem: The nurse evaluates complex cues and competing priorities while reviewing current assessment data in the medical-surgical unit. The client is a 49-year-old adult client. A client is found on the floor after attempting to ambulate without assistance. Which actions should the nurse take? Select all that apply.
rationale: After a fall, assess and treat the client, notify appropriate personnel, document factual findings, and complete internal safety reporting without referencing the report in the medical record. Distractors represent unsafe, nontherapeutic, delayed, unrelated, or non-priority actions/findings for this scenario.
options: Assess the client for injury before moving the client if injury is suspected.
Document in the chart that an incident report was completed.
Blame the assisting staff member in the progress note.
Document objective assessment findings in the medical record.
Move the client immediately before assessing for pain or deformity.
